bloodLoss.title: Blood Loss Details

spotting.title: Spotting Detected
spotting.description: Light bleeding between periods or at unexpected times. This may be normal but worth tracking for patterns.

severityDescriptions.very_light: Very light flow - minimal bleeding, may only need panty liners
severityDescriptions.light: Light flow - comfortable with regular pads or tampons
severityDescriptions.moderate: Moderate flow - normal menstrual bleeding, may need to change protection every 3-4 hours
severityDescriptions.heavy: Heavy flow - requires frequent changes of protection, may impact daily activities
severityDescriptions.very_heavy: Very heavy flow - may require super absorbent products, could significantly impact daily life

bloodLoss.trackingInfo: Tracking blood loss helps identify patterns and assess overall menstrual health. Share this data with your healthcare provider if you notice significant changes.

pain.title: Pain Assessment
pain.regionsWarning: Multiple pain regions may indicate widespread inflammation or condition progression.

levelDescriptions.2: Mild pain - noticeable but doesn't interfere with activities
levelDescriptions.3: Mild to moderate pain - some interference with daily activities
levelDescriptions.4: Moderate pain - interferes with concentration and daily tasks
levelDescriptions.5: Moderate to severe pain - significantly impacts daily activities
levelDescriptions.6: Severe pain - difficult to ignore, major impact on daily life
levelDescriptions.7: Very severe pain - dominates senses, difficult to think clearly
levelDescriptions.8: Intense pain - physical activity severely limited
levelDescriptions.9: Excruciating pain - unable to converse, crying out

pain.trackingInfo: Pain tracking helps identify patterns and triggers. This information is valuable for healthcare providers to understand your condition better.

mood.title: Mood Assessment

moodDescriptions.calm: Feeling peaceful and relaxed, free from stress or anxiety
moodDescriptions.happy: Experiencing joy, contentment, and positive emotions
moodDescriptions.excited: Feeling energetic, enthusiastic, and looking forward to activities
moodDescriptions.hopes: Feeling optimistic and looking forward to positive outcomes
moodDescriptions.confident: Feeling self-assured and capable of handling challenges
moodDescriptions.grateful: Feeling thankful and appreciative of positive aspects in life
moodDescriptions.anxious: Feeling worried, uneasy, or apprehensive about situations
moodDescriptions.stressed: Feeling overwhelmed, pressured, or unable to cope effectively
moodDescriptions.ashamed: Feeling embarrassed, guilty, or disappointed in oneself
moodDescriptions.angry: Feeling frustrated, irritated, or upset about circumstances
moodDescriptions.irritable: Feeling easily annoyed, impatient, or quick to anger
moodDescriptions.sad: Feeling down, melancholy, or experiencing low mood
moodDescriptions.mood_swings: Experiencing rapid or unpredictable changes in emotional state
moodDescriptions.worthless: Feeling inadequate, useless, or lacking in value
moodDescriptions.guilty: Feeling regretful, responsible for wrongdoing, or self-blame
moodDescriptions.overwhelmed: Feeling unable to cope with demands or responsibilities
moodDescriptions.hopeless: Feeling without hope, despairing, or pessimistic about the future
moodDescriptions.depressed: Experiencing persistent low mood, sadness, or lack of interest

mood.trackingInfo: Mood tracking helps identify emotional patterns related to your cycle and overall wellbeing.
mood.positiveMessage: Positive emotions can help with symptom management and overall wellbeing.
mood.negativeMessage: Challenging emotions are normal. Consider self-care strategies and support when needed.

trackingInsights.title: Mood Tracking Insights

balanceMessages.strongPositive: Strong positive day! This emotional state may help with symptom management and overall wellbeing.
balanceMessages.mostlyPositive: Mostly positive day with some challenges. This is a normal emotional balance.
balanceMessages.balanced: Balanced emotional day. You experienced both positive and challenging emotions.
balanceMessages.somewhatChallenging: Somewhat challenging day emotionally. Consider self-care strategies that have helped before.
balanceMessages.challenging: Emotionally challenging day. Remember that difficult emotions are temporary and support is available.

trackingInsights.correlationInfo: Mood patterns often correlate with physical symptoms. Share this information with your healthcare team to get comprehensive care.

impact.title: Daily Impact & Limitations

gradeDescriptions.10: Perfect day - no symptoms interfered with daily activities
gradeDescriptions.9: Excellent day - minimal symptom impact, felt very good
gradeDescriptions.8: Very good day - slight symptoms but managed well
gradeDescriptions.7: Good day - some symptoms but able to function normally
gradeDescriptions.6: Decent day - moderate symptoms with some limitations
gradeDescriptions.5: Average day - symptoms present but manageable
gradeDescriptions.4: Challenging day - symptoms significantly impacted activities
gradeDescriptions.3: Difficult day - symptoms made most activities challenging
gradeDescriptions.2: Very difficult day - symptoms severely limited activities
gradeDescriptions.1: Extremely difficult day - symptoms dominated the day

limitationDescriptions.used_medication: Needed medication to manage symptoms - indicates active symptom management
limitationDescriptions.missed_work: Unable to attend work due to symptoms - significant impact on professional life
limitationDescriptions.missed_school: Could not attend educational activities - academic impact from symptoms
limitationDescriptions.could_not_sport: Physical activities were limited - symptoms affected exercise capacity
limitationDescriptions.missed_social_activities: Social interactions were avoided - symptoms impacted social wellbeing
limitationDescriptions.missed_leisure_activities: Recreational activities were skipped - reduced quality of life
limitationDescriptions.had_to_sit_more: Required more sitting/resting - symptoms affected mobility and energy
limitationDescriptions.had_to_lie_down: Needed to lie down more than usual - significant symptom burden
limitationDescriptions.had_to_stay_longer_in_bed: Extended bed rest required - severe symptom impact
limitationDescriptions.could_not_do_unpaid_work: Household/volunteer work affected - daily functioning impaired
limitationDescriptions.other: Additional limitations not captured above - unique symptom impacts

impactLevels.minimal: Minimal - Symptoms had little to no effect on daily functioning.
impactLevels.mild: Mild - Some limitations but overall functioning maintained.
impactLevels.moderate: Moderate - Several areas of life affected by symptoms.
impactLevels.significant: Significant - Multiple daily activities were limited.
impactLevels.severe: Severe - Symptoms extensively affected daily functioning.

impact.noLimitationsMessage: Great! Your symptoms did not significantly interfere with your daily activities today.
impact.multipleLimitationsSummary: Multiple limitations suggest significant symptom burden. Consider discussing management strategies with your healthcare provider.
impact.trackingInfo: Impact tracking helps quantify how symptoms affect your daily life and activities.

generalHealth.title: General Health & Energy

energyLevels.1: Very Low - Exhausted, barely able to function
energyLevels.2: Low - Tired, limited energy for activities
energyLevels.3: Moderate - Average energy, can handle daily tasks
energyLevels.4: Good - Above average energy, feeling capable

energyDescriptions.1: Very Low Energy - Feeling extremely tired, may need to rest frequently and avoid strenuous activities
energyDescriptions.2: Low Energy - Below normal energy levels, some activities may feel challenging or require more effort
energyDescriptions.3: Moderate Energy - Average energy levels, able to handle daily tasks with normal effort
energyDescriptions.4: Good Energy - Above average energy, feeling capable and able to engage in most activities comfortably
energyDescriptions.5: High Energy - Excellent energy levels, feeling vibrant and able to tackle demanding activities

symptomDescriptions.fatigue: Persistent tiredness that doesn't improve with rest, often affecting daily functioning
symptomDescriptions.headache: Head pain that may range from mild discomfort to severe, debilitating pain
symptomDescriptions.nausea: Feeling of sickness with an inclination to vomit, may affect appetite and daily activities
symptomDescriptions.dizziness: Sensation of unsteadiness or spinning that may affect balance and coordination
symptomDescriptions.weakness: Reduced strength or energy affecting physical capabilities and endurance
symptomDescriptions.joint_pain: Discomfort or pain in joints that may limit movement and daily activities
symptomDescriptions.muscle_pain: Aching or soreness in muscles that may affect mobility and comfort
symptomDescriptions.fever: Elevated body temperature indicating possible infection or inflammation
symptomDescriptions.chills: Feeling cold or shivering, often accompanying fever or illness
symptomDescriptions.sweating: Excessive perspiration that may indicate hormonal changes or illness
symptomDescriptions.bloated: Feeling of fullness or swelling in the abdomen, often related to digestive issues
symptomDescriptions.painful_sensitive_breasts: Breast tenderness or pain, often related to hormonal changes
symptomDescriptions.acne: Skin breakouts that may be related to hormonal fluctuations or stress
symptomDescriptions.muscle_joint_pain: Combined muscle and joint discomfort affecting mobility and comfort
symptomDescriptions.headache_migraine: Severe headache that may include sensitivity to light, sound, or nausea

correlationMessages.lowEnergyWithSymptoms: Your low energy level combined with symptoms suggests significant health impact today.
correlationMessages.goodEnergyWithSymptoms: Despite symptoms, your good energy level shows resilience and effective coping.
correlationMessages.lowEnergyNoSymptoms: Low energy without other symptoms may indicate need for rest or lifestyle adjustments.
correlationMessages.goodEnergyNoSymptoms: Excellent! High energy with no symptoms indicates optimal health management.
correlationMessages.moderate: Your energy and symptom levels suggest a manageable health day.

insightMessages.goodEnergy: Good energy levels support better symptom management, improved mood, and enhanced quality of life.
insightMessages.lowEnergy: Low energy may indicate increased disease activity or treatment side effects.
insightMessages.moderate: Moderate energy levels are manageable but may benefit from lifestyle adjustments.

generalHealth.noSymptomsMessage: Great! No symptoms reported today indicates good health management.
generalHealth.noSymptomsSummary: Symptom-free days are important markers of good health management and treatment effectiveness.
generalHealth.multipleSymptomsWarning: Multiple symptoms may indicate increased disease activity or treatment side effects.
generalHealth.trackingInfo: Energy levels often correlate with symptom burden. Tracking both helps identify patterns and treatment effectiveness.

diet.title: Diet Items
diet.beneficialSummary: These foods support overall health and may help manage symptoms through their nutritional benefits.
diet.negativeSummary: These items may contribute to inflammation or worsen symptoms in some individuals. Consider moderation or alternatives.
diet.neutralSummary: These foods have neutral effects on your symptoms but still contribute to overall nutrition.

descriptions.vegetables: Rich in vitamins, minerals, and fiber. Essential for digestive health and inflammation reduction.
descriptions.fruit: Natural source of vitamins, antioxidants, and fiber. Supports immune system and provides natural energy.
descriptions.potato_rice_bread: Complex carbohydrates provide sustained energy. Choose whole grains when possible.
descriptions.dairy_products: Good source of calcium and protein. May affect inflammation in some individuals.
descriptions.nuts_tofu_tempe: Plant-based proteins with healthy fats. Anti-inflammatory properties may help with symptoms.
descriptions.egg: Complete protein source with essential amino acids. Generally well-tolerated by most people.
descriptions.fish: Omega-3 fatty acids have anti-inflammatory properties. Excellent for overall health.
descriptions.meat: High-quality protein source. Red meat may increase inflammation in some people.
descriptions.snacks: Processed foods may contribute to inflammation. Choose healthier alternatives when possible.
descriptions.water: Essential for hydration and overall health. Helps with symptom management and medication effectiveness.
descriptions.coffee: Contains antioxidants but caffeine may affect some symptoms. Monitor individual response.
descriptions.alcohol: May interfere with medications and worsen symptoms. Consider limiting intake.

diet.trackingInfo: Diet tracking helps identify foods that may influence your symptoms and overall wellbeing.

stoolUrine.title: Stool & Urine Conditions

bloodMessages.urine: Blood detected in urine may indicate urinary tract issues or other conditions requiring medical evaluation.
bloodMessages.stool: Blood in stool can indicate digestive tract issues and should be evaluated by a healthcare provider.
bloodMessages.both: Blood detected in both urine and stool requires prompt medical attention for proper evaluation.

consistencyDescriptions.hard: Hard, difficult to pass stools may indicate constipation or dehydration. Consider increasing fiber and water intake.
consistencyDescriptions.normal: Normal, well-formed stools indicate healthy digestive function and good hydration.
consistencyDescriptions.soft: Soft stools are generally normal but monitor for changes that might indicate digestive issues.
consistencyDescriptions.watery: Watery stools may indicate diarrhea, which can lead to dehydration if persistent.
consistencyDescriptions.something_else: Unusual stool characteristics may warrant further observation or medical consultation.
consistencyDescriptions.no_stool: No bowel movement today. Monitor for patterns of constipation or changes in bowel habits.

stoolUrine.bloodWarning: Blood detection warrants medical evaluation. Contact your healthcare provider to discuss these findings.
stoolUrine.normalMessage: Normal digestive function supports overall health and medication absorption.
stoolUrine.trackingInfo: Digestive health tracking helps identify patterns that may be related to your condition or treatments.

sleep.title: Sleep Quality

issueDescriptions.trouble_asleep: Difficulty initiating sleep may be caused by stress, pain, caffeine, or irregular sleep schedules.
issueDescriptions.wake_up_during_night: Sleep fragmentation can reduce sleep quality and may be related to pain, anxiety, or environmental factors.
issueDescriptions.not_tired_rested: Feeling unrefreshed despite sleep may indicate poor sleep quality or underlying sleep disorders.

durationMessages.optimal: Your sleep duration falls within the recommended 7-9 hours for adults.
durationMessages.short: Less than 6 hours may not provide adequate rest for optimal health and symptom management.
durationMessages.long: More than 10 hours may indicate underlying health issues or medication effects.
durationMessages.borderline: Consider aiming for 7-9 hours for optimal health benefits.

sleep.qualityMessage: Quality sleep supports immune function, pain management, and emotional wellbeing.
sleep.poorSleepWarning: Poor sleep can worsen symptoms and affect overall health. Consider discussing sleep hygiene strategies with your healthcare provider.
sleep.trackingInfo: Sleep patterns can significantly impact symptom severity, mood, and treatment effectiveness. Consistent, quality sleep is crucial for managing chronic conditions.

exercise.title: Exercise Activity

typeDescriptions.high_impact: High-impact exercises like running or jumping. Great for cardiovascular health and bone density, but may increase joint stress.
typeDescriptions.low_impact: Low-impact activities like walking or swimming. Gentle on joints while providing cardiovascular and strength benefits.
typeDescriptions.precision_exercise: Controlled, precise movements that focus on form and technique. Excellent for rehabilitation and targeted strengthening.

benefitMessages.extended: Over 60 minutes of exercise provides excellent cardiovascular and mental health benefits.
benefitMessages.moderate: 30-60 minutes provides substantial health benefits and is ideal for most fitness goals.
benefitMessages.light: Even short exercise sessions provide health benefits and can help manage symptoms.

exercise.noExerciseMessage: Rest days are important for recovery and preventing overexertion.
exercise.noExerciseAdvice: Consider gentle activities like stretching or short walks when feeling up to it. Always listen to your body and consult your healthcare provider about appropriate exercise levels.
exercise.exerciseBenefits: Regular exercise can help reduce inflammation, improve mood, enhance sleep quality, and may help manage chronic condition symptoms.
exercise.trackingBenefits: Tracking exercise helps identify which activities you tolerate well and which might trigger symptoms. This information is valuable for creating a personalized exercise plan.
exercise.restDayBenefits: Both active days and rest days are important data points for understanding your activity tolerance and symptom patterns.

sexualHealth.title: Sexual Health

issueDescriptions.pain: Pain during sexual activity may indicate underlying conditions that warrant medical evaluation.
issueDescriptions.discomfort: Discomfort can affect intimacy and may be related to your health condition or treatments.
issueDescriptions.bleeding: Bleeding during or after sexual activity should be evaluated by a healthcare provider.
issueDescriptions.dryness: Vaginal dryness can be caused by hormonal changes, medications, or medical conditions.
issueDescriptions.fatigue: Fatigue affecting sexual activity may be related to your overall health condition.

experienceMessages.positive: Satisfying sexual activity contributes to overall wellbeing and relationship health.
experienceMessages.negative: Unsatisfying experiences may be related to physical symptoms, medications, or emotional factors.
experienceMessages.avoided: Avoidance may be due to physical discomfort, fatigue, or concern about symptoms.
experienceMessages.noActivity: Sexual activity patterns can be affected by health conditions, treatments, and overall wellbeing.

sexualHealth.avoidanceMessage: Sexual activity was avoided today, which may be related to symptoms or health concerns.
sexualHealth.issuesWarning: Sexual health concerns can significantly impact quality of life. Consider discussing these issues with your healthcare provider for appropriate support and treatment options.
sexualHealth.trackingInfo: Sexual health is an important component of overall health and quality of life. Tracking patterns helps identify how your condition affects intimacy and can guide discussions with healthcare providers.

notes.title: Personal Notes

valueMessages.withNote: Recording personal notes helps create a comprehensive health picture.
valueMessages.withoutNote: Consider adding notes: Personal observations can be incredibly valuable for health tracking.

benefitMessages.withNote: Your notes provide valuable context that numbers alone cannot capture. They help healthcare providers understand your daily experience and can reveal important patterns over time.
benefitMessages.withoutNote: Notes can include how you felt, what helped or worsened symptoms, medication effects, or any other observations. This qualitative data complements your quantitative health metrics.

tips: Record specific symptoms and their severity
Note what helped or worsened your condition
Include medication effects or side effects
Be honest: Accurate notes lead to better care
